title: Substance Use Disorders: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Substance Use Disorders
Identify key diagnostic features of Substance Use Disorders
Apply clinical reasoning to Substance Use Disorders management
Recognize common pitfalls in Substance Use Disorders

sections.title: Overview of Substance Use Disorders
sections.content:
**Substance Use Disorders**



**Key Points:**
• Alcohol withdrawal timeline: 6-24h tremors/anxiety → 12-48h seizures → 48-96h delirium tremens (mortality 5-15%)
• DT features: confusion, agitation, hallucinations, autonomic instability, seizures
• Treatment: benzodiazepines (CIWA protocol), thiamine BEFORE glucose, supportive care

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Alcohol withdrawal timeline: 6-24h tremors/anxiety → 12-48h seizures → 48-96h delirium tremens (mortality 5-15%)
• DT features: confusion, agitation, hallucinations, autonomic instability, seizures

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Treatment: benzodiazepines (CIWA protocol), thiamine BEFORE glucose, supportive care
• Opioid withdrawal: NOT life-threatening (unlike alcohol/benzo withdrawal); treat with buprenorphine or methadone
• Screening: CAGE questionnaire (2+ positive = concerning); AUDIT for alcohol use severity

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Substance Use Disorders:**

• Treatment: benzodiazepines (CIWA protocol), thiamine BEFORE glucose, supportive care
• Opioid withdrawal: NOT life-threatening (unlike alcohol/benzo withdrawal); treat with buprenorphine or methadone
• Screening: CAGE questionnaire (2+ positive = concerning); AUDIT for alcohol use severity

mcqs.stem: A 22-year-old college student presents with 5 days of decreased sleep (2 hours/night with no fatigue), pressured speech, grandiose plans to start three companies, and $15,000 in impulsive purchases. Which diagnosis and initial treatment are most appropriate?

options.text: Bipolar I disorder, manic episode — start lithium or valproate plus an atypical antipsychotic
options.explanation: This meets DSM-5 criteria for a manic episode: ≥1 week of elevated/expansive mood with ≥3 symptoms (decreased need for sleep, pressured speech, grandiosity, impulsive spending). Acute mania treatment per APA guidelines includes mood stabilizer (lithium or valproate) plus atypical antipsychotic (olanzapine, quetiapine, or risperidone) for rapid symptom control.

options.text: Substance-induced mood disorder — observe and toxicology screen only
options.explanation: While substance use should always be ruled out, a 5-day sustained presentation with classic manic symptoms should not be attributed to substances without evidence. Treatment should not be delayed pending a negative tox screen.

options.text: ADHD with comorbid anxiety — start stimulant medication
options.explanation: While ADHD can present with impulsivity and pressured speech, the episodic nature, decreased need for sleep without fatigue, and grandiosity are characteristic of mania. Stimulants can worsen manic symptoms.

options.text: Bipolar II disorder, hypomanic episode — start an SSRI
options.explanation: Hypomania by definition does NOT cause marked impairment or require hospitalization. $15,000 in impulsive purchases and severe functional impairment indicate full mania (Bipolar I). SSRIs can trigger or worsen mania and are not first-line.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Bipolar I disorder, manic episode — start lithium or valproate plus an atypical antipsychotic.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Bipolar I disorder, manic episode — start lithium or valproate plus an atypical antipsychotic

coachDx.misconception: While substance use should always be ruled out, a 5-day sustained presentation with classic manic symptoms should not be attributed to substances without evidence. Treatment should not be delayed pend
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Bipolar I disorder, manic episode — start lithium or valproate plus an atypical  from Substance-induced mood disorder — observe and toxicology screen only.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Bipolar I disorder, manic episode — start lithium or valproate plus an atypical antipsychotic.
